## [00:00 - 01:33]

Law 28 will help the majority of people. I see so many DMs online. I see so many comments of people who they want to take action, but they either don't or they take action, but they're tiptoeing around it and they don't do it in a bold way. There's power behind taking bold action. How would you define bold action for people out there versus just normal, unboldened action? When it scares you a little bit, when taking that action makes your heart beat faster. When you take bold action, you trick yourself into feeling more brave. You look more brave and you make other people think you're more brave, which is powerful. It inspires the people around you. It creates momentum. It creates excitement. It intimidates others. It raises doubt if you have any. And it also erases the doubts of the people around you if they have any. And being bold also makes you stand out. I mean, far out when I was kind of noting down all the benefits of being bold, that's pretty cool. I know that a lot of my friends were giving me because when I got into archery, I went out to the archery store and was so bold because I said, give me the best Olympian bow that you have in the store. And I was like, no, no, no, man, that's crazy. Are you sure? And I'm like, yeah, I want the best fricking bow that inspired a bunch of friends of mine here in Adelaide as well to get into archery because I was like, oh, why that's so cool. That created momentum for me in archery also helped me get more excited about it. And then now also because I have such an incredible bow, I feel more drawn to actually go take it out and then shoot the bow, right? There is a lot of power in taking really bold action.
